title.purpose: Modal and section headers; the size ceiling (15px).

body.purpose: Default UI text and ticket prose (14px / 1.55).

shadows.name: ambient-lift
shadows.purpose: Subtle lift on raised-but-inline elements: active tabs, success cards.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Modals only — floats the dialog above a dimmed room.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions (the --ease-out token).

motion.name: state-feedback
motion.purpose: Duration for interactive state changes (seam lift, hover).

motion.name: empty-enter
motion.purpose: Fade-up on empty-state entry; disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single key action per surface (Open, Resume). Teal fill, dark ink.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--teal, oklch(0.78 0.085 168)); color: var(--bg, oklch(0.21 0.012 165)); border: 0; border-radius: 8px; padding: 6px 14px; font: 600 13px "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.06); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Lower-priority / destructive-adjacent action (Stop). Ghost with hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: var(--ink-soft, oklch(0.82 0.012 120)); border: 1px solid var(--hairline-strong, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.14)); border-radius: 8px; padding: 6px 14px; font: 600 13px "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { color: var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: The ticket-key opener and config fields. Surface fill, teal focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { background: var(--surface, oklch(0.255 0.014 165)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); border: 1px solid var(--hairline-strong, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.14)); border-radius: 8px; padding: 6px 10px; font: 400 14px "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--ink-muted, oklch(0.70 0.014 140)); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--teal, oklch(0.78 0.085 168)); outline-offset: 1px; border-color: transparent; }

components.name: Tab
components.description: Ticket / session tabs. Top-rounded, sits into the panel below; active lifts to surface-2.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; gap: 4px; } .ds-tab { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; background: var(--surface, oklch(0.255 0.014 165)); color: var(--ink-soft, oklch(0.82 0.012 120)); border: 1px solid var(--hairline, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.08)); border-bottom: 0; border-radius: 8px 8px 0 0; padding: 5px 10px; font: 600 13px "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; } .ds-tab--active { background: var(--surface-2, oklch(0.305 0.016 165)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); box-shadow: 0 1px 3px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.3); } .ds-tab__x { color: var(--ink-muted, oklch(0.70 0.014 140)); } .ds-tab:hover .ds-tab__x { color: var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); }

components.name: Success (PR) Card
components.description: Surfaced when a YOLO run opens a PR/MR. The one place green tints a surface.
components.css: .ds-pr-card { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 10px; padding: 8px 12px; background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--green, oklch(0.79 0.11 155)) 14%, var(--surface, oklch(0.255 0.014 165))); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--green, oklch(0.79 0.11 155)) 40%, var(--hairline-strong, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.14))); border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.3); } .ds-pr-card__label { color: var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); font: 600 13px "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-pr-card__open { background: var(--teal, oklch(0.78 0.085 168)); color: var(--bg, oklch(0.21 0.012 165)); border: 0; border-radius: 8px; padding: 5px 14px; font: 600 13px "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; } .ds-pr-card__open:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Modal
components.description: Config editors, About, confirm gates. surface-2 dialog with Overlay shadow over a scrim.
components.html:
About SeniorDev

Application name, version, and credits.
components.css: .ds-modal { display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-width: 420px; max-width: min(860px, 92vw); max-height: 88vh; background: var(--surface-2, oklch(0.305 0.016 165)); color: var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); border: 1px solid var(--hairline-strong, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.14)); border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 20px 60px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.45); } .ds-modal__head { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 12px 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--hairline, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.08)); } .ds-modal__head h2 { margin: 0; font: 600 15px "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-modal__x { background: transparent; border: 0; color: var(--ink-muted, oklch(0.70 0.014 140)); font-size: 18px; cursor: pointer; } .ds-modal__x:hover { color: var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); } .ds-modal__body { padding: 14px 16px; font: 400 14px "Segoe UI Variable Text", "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif; }

components.name: YOLO Log Line
components.description: The machine's voice — headless agent output and tool one-liners in Consolas mono.
components.html:
▸ Edit src/foo.ts
▸ Bash pnpm test
✅ PR ready — https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42
components.css: .ds-log { margin: 0; padding: 8px 10px; background: #1a1f1d; color: var(--ink, oklch(0.95 0.008 95)); border-radius: 8px; font: 400 13px Consolas, monospace; line-height: 1.4; white-space: pre-wrap; }

narrative.overview: SeniorDev is a dark, legible operations surface where a developer supervises machine work. Two tabbed panels — a Jira ticket reader and a live CLI-agent terminal multiplexer — sit side by side, and every autonomous run is watchable and traceable: a scrolling log, tool one-liners, PR cards, a structured recap. The room is dim so the work is bright. Surfaces are a warm near-neutral charcoal, text is a warm off-white, and a single restrained teal accent marks the one action that matters on any surface. The system reads as capable, quietly confident, and transparent — a senior teammate, not a control panel. It rejects the enterprise dashboard, the toy, the maximal IDE, and the flashy. Components carry a tactile, confident weight without ever raising their voice. Depth is built from tone before shadow: three flat charcoal planes stack by lightness, hairlines draw the seams, and a soft ambient shadow lifts genuinely-raised elements.
keyCharacteristics: Dark warm-charcoal canvas, warm off-white ink, one rare teal accent.
OKLCH throughout — the palette is authored and reasoned in OKLCH, not hex.
Flat tonal layering (bg/surface/surface-2) plus hairlines; shadow used sparingly for lift.
Semantic color vocabulary: teal (primary/active), green (success), amber (attention), rust (error).
Consumer sans for UI (Segoe UI Variable), Consolas mono for all terminal/log surfaces.
Density where the task earns it; calm everywhere else.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Teal marks exactly one primary action per surface. If two teal buttons compete on a screen, one is wrong — demote it to the secondary (ghost) treatment.

rules.name: The Color-Is-State Rule
rules.body: Green, amber, and rust may only appear as state (success / attention / error), never as decoration or brand flavor. State is never conveyed by color alone — always pair it with text, icon, or shape.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Type sizes are fixed px/rem, never clamp(). Users view at consistent DPI; a fluid heading that shrinks in a panel is a regression, not a feature.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Segoe UI Variable for everything the human reads; Consolas for everything the machine emits. No third face, no display font in UI chrome.

rules.name: The Tone-First Rule
rules.body: Reach for a lighter plane before a shadow. Shadow is for lift and overlay, not for separating adjacent panels — that's the hairline's job.

dos: Do author every color in OKLCH; the frontmatter values are the source of truth.
Do keep teal to one primary action per surface (The One Signal Rule).
Do convey depth with the tonal planes (bg/surface/surface-2) and hairlines first; reach for Ambient Lift only to raise a card or active tab.
Do give every interactive element a visible outline focus state — the tool is keyboard-first.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion on every animation with a crossfade or instant fallback.
Do pair state color with text/icon/shape; never rely on color alone.
Do keep type at fixed px sizes and a single sans; Consolas for machine output.
Do verify ink-muted body/placeholder text clears 4.5:1 on its plane.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no card grids, hero-metric tiles, or gradient accents.
Don't make it consumer-cute or toy-like — the raccoon is a moment, not a theme.
Don't pile on heavy IDE chrome: no cluttered toolbars, deeply nested panels, or config-overload.
Don't add flashy or over-animated flourishes: no choreographed page loads, decorative motion, or glassmorphism.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient text, or a colored border-left/border-right > 1px as a stripe accent.
Don't use green/amber/rust as decoration — they are state only.
Don't use clamp() / fluid type or a display font anywhere in UI chrome.
Don't reach for a modal first; exhaust inline and progressive alternatives.
